headline: Samsung Electronics ships faster HBM4E chip samples to customers; shares jump
summary: Samsung Electronics on Friday said it started shipping samples of its latest high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chip to customers, pulling ahead of rivals in distributing a new version of the product critical to ‌AI data centers and sending its shares higher.  Samsung said the chip uses its latest 1c DRAM process technology - sixth-generation, 10-nanometer-class DRAM - alongside Samsung's 4-nanometer foundry ​logic base die.  The rollout is part of Samsung's efforts to regain momentum in the HBM market after falling behind rivals like SK Hynix and Micron in supplying advanced artificial intelligence memory chips, particularly to Nvidia.

content.title: Samsung Electronics ships faster HBM4E chip samples to customers; shares jump
content.summary: Samsung Electronics on Friday said it started shipping samples of its latest high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chip to customers, pulling ahead of rivals in distributing a new version of the product critical to ‌AI data centers and sending its shares higher.  Samsung said the chip uses its latest 1c DRAM process technology - sixth-generation, 10-nanometer-class DRAM - alongside Samsung's 4-nanometer foundry ​logic base die.  The rollout is part of Samsung's efforts to regain momentum in the HBM market after falling behind rivals like SK Hynix and Micron in supplying advanced artificial intelligence memory chips, particularly to Nvidia.

headline: The Week in Numbers: Temu fined, Micron's massive valuation
summary:
STORY: From a big fine for Temu to Micron's massive valuation.

:: Micron

:: The Week In Numbers

This is The Week in Numbers.

:: $232 million

$232 million dollars is how much Chinese online retailer Temu has been fined by the European Union.

EU tech regulators say the company failed to stop the sale of illegal products under the Digital Services Act.

The Act requires large online companies to do more to tackle illegal and harmful content on their platforms.

In a statement Temu says it respects the DSA objectives but disagrees with the Commission's decision. 

:: $1.43 billion

$1.43 billion is the amount of damages the Australian government is seeking in its lawsuit against U.S. chemical maker 3M.

That's after its firefighting foam it supplied contained so-called "forever chemicals'.

It's the country's largest ever legal claim after the foam was used at 28 defense bases across the country. 

3M said it would defend itself against the claims in court. 

:: $150 billion

$150 billion a year is what Nvidia's chief executive said the chip company plans to invest in Taiwan.

CEO Jensen Huang termed it the "epicenter" of the AI revolution and predicted it will be the world's tech manufacturing hub for a long time.

His comments came at the launch celebration in Taipei for the chipmaker's planned Taiwan headquarters.

:: $1 trillion

:: Micron

$1 trillion is the market value chipmaker Micron Technology topped for the first time.

It crowns a dizzying rally that has cemented the largest U.S. memory chipmaker as one of the standout winners of the AI boom. 

Micron's shares rose more than 18% on Tuesday, pushing the stock to a record high.

:: 8%

:: Ferrari

8% is the plunge Ferrari shares took on Tuesday after the auto icon unveiled it's first electric vehicle.

The five-seat sedan bears little resemblance to Ferraris of the past, drawing a cool response from fans, and investors. 

CEO Benedetto Vigna defended the new model and said the company is receiving "strong interest" from new and existing customers.

The five-seat sedan bears little resemblance to Ferraris of the past, drawing a cool response from fans, and investors. 

CEO Benedetto Vigna defended the new model and said the company is receiving "strong interest" from new and existing customers.

headline: Samsung ships faster HBM4E chip samples to customers; shares jump
summary:
STORY: Samsung Electronics made a big move in the AI chip race on Friday.

It said it's started shipping samples of its latest high-bandwidth memory - or HBM - chip to customers.

It pulls the South Korean tech giant ahead of rivals as they compete to send out new versions of HBMs which are critical to AI data centers.

The new chip is the 12-layer HBM4E and Samsung said it's more than 20% faster than its previous-generation HBM4 products.

The rollout is part of Samsung's efforts to regain momentum in the HBM market.

It had fallen behind rivals like SK Hynix and Micron in supplying advanced AI memory chips, particularly to Nvidia.

Samsung's clients include major AI players Nvidia, AMD and Google, among others.

Demand has surged for advanced memory chips used in AI servers and processors.

Investors liked what they heard Friday.

Shares of Samsung Electronics rose as much as 6.5% in morning trading.

Rival SK Hynix also saw its shares climb just over 1%.

The rollout is part of Samsung's efforts to regain momentum in the HBM market.

headline: AI server sales push Dell stock to new heights: Earnings takeaways
summary: Dell Technologies (DELL) stock is on the move in Friday's pre-market trading as its share price soars by as much as nearly 40% — bringing the share price into the $400 range — after reporting a booming $60 billion in AI server sales for the company's first quarter. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland dive into Dell's earnings results and how its AI demand-driven growth compares to that seen in the software sector.

content.title: AI server sales push Dell stock to new heights: Earnings takeaways
content.description:
Dell Technologies (DELL) stock is on the move in Friday's pre-market trading as its share price soars by as much as nearly 40% — bringing the share price into the $400 range — after reporting a booming $60 billion in AI server sales for the company's first quarter.

Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland dive into Dell's earnings results and how its AI demand-driven growth compares to that seen in the software sector.

headline: Nvidia GTC Hype vs Stock Reaction: Why Isn't $NVDA Moving More?
summary: Despite Jensen Huang's announcements at Nvidia GTC, including projections of doubling chip demand and a trillion-dollar market, Nvidia's stock has remained range-bound. Shy Balore explains the market's focus on inference economics and the need for consumer AI products to drive the next leg of growth. He also analyzes Micron's HBM4 production and its strong gross margins, suggesting a durable pricing power in the memory market.

He also analyzes Micron's HBM4 production and its strong gross margins, suggesting a durable pricing power in the memory market.

headline: Micron Isn’t Nvidia. It’s Time to Take Your Foot Off the Gas with MU Stock.
summary: Investors pricing in a decade of dominance for Micron are falling into a dangerous trap by ignoring the physical realities of the semiconductor market. The company is selling standardized hardware, and once competitors scale their own production using the exact same ASML technology, pricing power will collapse.

content.title: Micron Isn’t Nvidia. It’s Time to Take Your Foot Off the Gas with MU Stock.

headline: Three Semi Stocks Hit $1 TLN...But That's Just the Start
summary: SK Hynix joined Samsung Electronics Co. and Micron Technology Inc. (MU) in the $1 trillion market cap club this week. The valuation crowd and spreadsheet gang are going bonkers. Let ‘em, says Keith Fitz-Gerald, editor of 5 With Fitz.
